ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general coffee beverage class but no specific entry for Vietnamese coffee with condensed milk and caramel flan combination. Closest defensible anchor used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers coffee with milk as the closest defensible match. No specific code exists for the Vietnamese condensed-milk coffee plus caramel flan combination dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Vietnamese coffee-dessert combination. Left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.description: 1 serving of coffee with 1 piece of flan
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving consists of approximately 300 g total — a glass of Vietnamese drip coffee with condensed milk (approximately 200 g) paired with one individual caramel flan portion (approximately 100 g).

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cafe au lait (coffee with milk) and Flan, caramel custard component values; weighted composite estimate
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for the Vietnamese coffee-with-condensed-milk-plus-caramel-flan combination. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of: (1) USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 174878 — Beverages, coffee, instant, with whitener and low-calorie sweetener; (2) USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 168833 — Sweetened condensed milk; (3) USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 169235 — Flan, caramel custard. The input record values (energy 116.67 kcal, protein 2.67 g, fat 5.0 g, carbohydrate 15.0 g per 100 g) are consistent with a ~2:1 coffee-to-flan ratio and are retained as the best available estimate. Singapore HPB and FAO/INFOODS SEA tables do not contain a matching entry for this specific combination.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on sweetened condensed milk coffee and caramel custard
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Vietnamese coffee with condensed milk and caramel flan. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on: (1) sweetened condensed milk GI ~61 (University of Sydney GI Database); (2) caramel custard / flan GI ~65–68 (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care); (3) coffee itself has negligible GI impact. Weighted average of the two carbohydrate-contributing components yields an estimated GI of ~65. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (65 × 45 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 29.2 g, classified as high GL.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (13.33 g/100 g) with zero dietary fibre; caffeine from Vietnamese robusta coffee
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this beverage-dessert combination: the 13.33 g/100 g sugar load with zero fibre drives insulin-mediated sodium retention and independently raises blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines, while the caffeine content of Vietnamese robusta coffee can cause acute transient blood pressure elevation; if consumed, limit to one small serving and substitute condensed milk with unsweetened evaporated milk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar (13.33 g/100 g), zero dietary fibre, estimated GI 65, high glycemic load per serving (~29 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: This combination is unsuitable for blood glucose management in individuals with Type 2 Diabetes: the estimated GI of 65 combined with a glycemic load of approximately 29 per standard 300 g serving represents a high glycaemic impact, driven by concentrated sugars from condensed milk and caramel flan with no fibre to attenuate absorption; patients should avoid this item or replace condensed milk with a non-caloric sweetener and reduce flan portion size significantly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (2.67 g/100 g), cholesterol (60 mg/100 g), trans fat (0.07 g/100 g), and high added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this item due to its saturated fat (2.67 g/100 g from condensed milk and egg yolks), cholesterol (60 mg/100 g), and trace trans fat (0.07 g/100 g), all of which contribute to atherogenic risk; the high added sugar load (13.33 g/100 g) further raises triglyceride levels per AHA dietary guidelines, and occasional consumption should be paired with an otherwise low-saturated-fat dietary pattern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar (13.33 g/100 g) with zero fibre promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine production; saturated fat from condensed milk and eggs
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this combination: the 13.33 g/100 g sugar content with zero dietary fibre promotes inflammatory mediator production, and the saturated fat from condensed milk and egg yolks contributes to low-grade systemic inflammation; coffee's chlorogenic acids provide insufficient anti-inflammatory offset at this sugar and fat load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cà phê sữa bánh flan (Vietnamese Coffee with Condensed Milk and Caramel Flan) is a popular Vietnamese dessert-and-beverage combination served widely in Vietnamese cafes and food delivery platforms across Southeast Asia, including Singapore. The dish pairs a glass of strong Vietnamese drip coffee (typically brewed from dark-roasted robusta beans using a traditional phin filter) mixed with sweetened condensed milk, alongside an individual portion of silky caramel flan (bánh flan) — a steamed or baked egg custard dessert topped with amber caramel sauce. The coffee component delivers a bold, slightly bitter flavour with a rich, sweet creaminess from the condensed milk, while the flan contributes a smooth, wobbly, mildly sweet egg-custard texture with a bittersweet caramel note. Together, the combination provides approximately 116.67 kcal per 100 g, with 2.67 g protein, 5.0 g fat (2.67 g saturated), 15.0 g carbohydrate, and 13.33 g sugar. A standard 300 g serving delivers approximately 350 kcal and a glycemic load of ~29, classifying it as a high-GL item. The high sugar content (13.33 g/100 g) with zero dietary fibre makes this combination clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. Calcium content is notable at 66.67 mg/100 g from the dairy components. This item is vegetarian and gluten-free but contains milk and egg allergens.

confidence_reason: No single approved source entry exists for this specific Vietnamese coffee-with-condensed-milk-plus-caramel-flan combination. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central entries for sweetened condensed milk, coffee with milk, and caramel custard components. The input record values are internally consistent with the expected macronutrient profile for a ~2:1 coffee-to-flan ratio and are retained as the best available estimate. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are a weighted composite estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central component entries (sweetened condensed milk FDC 168833, coffee with milk FDC 174878, caramel custard FDC 169235) as no single approved-source entry exists for this specific combination. Input record values retained as consistent with expected profile. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, B-vitamin profile, and moisture data for this composite Vietnamese coffee-flan preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as a combined dish entry as of 2026. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero: coffee beverages and egg-based caramel custard contain negligible dietary fibre; this is consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for both components. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero: neither coffee with condensed milk nor caramel flan contains meaningful vitamin C per USDA FoodData Central. 4. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific combination was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. The food_name has been corrected from the input 'Caphe sữa bánh plan' (apparent OCR/transliteration error for 'flan') to the canonical English name 'Vietnamese Coffee with Condensed Milk and Caramel Flan', with the Vietnamese local name 'Cà phê sữa bánh flan'. 6. country_of_origin in the input was listed as Singapore; this has been corrected to Vietnam as the dish's culinary origin, though it is widely served in Singapore.
